name: sprint-frame-picker
description: One-shot variant of the canonical sprint pipeline for the EnsembleWorks Frames-drawer feature. Skips persona-interview + dogfood (no personas; not a pipeline tool), swaps executable acceptance tests for an agent-driven walkthrough (this repo has no acceptance harness), and adds a multi-model code-quality gate before the human bossman gate.

inputs.goal: Add fast frame navigation: a caret on the left of the current page's side-panel section flies a Frames drawer out to the left; hover peeks, click pins (persists); clicking a frame flies the camera there.

steps.description: Enumerate acceptance criteria as a CHECKLIST (this repo has no executable acceptance harness — do not fabricate one). Design-first: every user-facing flow of the drawer walked end-to-end against the v5 prototype.

gate.prompt: Approve the AC checklist. Is every drawer flow specified end-to-end and objectively checkable?

retro.lesson: When a repo doesn't do executable acceptance tests our normal way, enumerate ACs as a checklist and verify with an agent-driven walkthrough of the live app — match the repo's conventions, don't import ceremony.

steps.description: Mechanical pre-build gates: the new unit tests fail correctly (RED-is-real, assertion on the real expected value, not a missing module) and every checklist item is objectively checkable.
The v5 interactive artifact is the seed (5 rounds, user-approved). Produce the final before/after for the walkthrough.
Plan the slice against the reuse map (focus.ts jump, panelLayout store pattern, CommandBar accelerator guard, PanelPages section header).
Adversarial fresh-context review of the plan: refute every file:line reuse claim against the real code; flag over-engineering vs the deliberately-minimal v1 scope.

TDD: Navigator writes the failing node:assert unit tests (store + pure helpers), Driver implements; then the tldraw-touching component + wiring. Match repo conventions exactly (inline wm tokens, data-testid, useValue, no new deps, no .tsx tests).
Test-pyramid + no-mock + test-first-commit checks; typecheck + build green across all workspaces.

NEW — agent-driven acceptance: drive the live app (bin/dev up, seed session layout) through every item in docs/acceptance/frames-drawer.md, recording PASS/FAIL + screenshot/log evidence per line. Replaces executable acceptance tests for this repo.

NEW — multi-model quality gate: three independent judges on DIFFERENT models (claude-opus-4-8, gemini, codex) each score the diff + tests + neighbor files against a repo-derived rubric and answer 'at least as high quality as the surrounding code?'. Rubric: naming/altitude match neighbors; tests indistinguishable from panelLayout.test.ts / roomHooks.test.ts (node:assert, under src/, no mock lib, pure logic extracted, duck-typed fake editor); inline wm tokens + data-testid + useValue; no new deps; no .tsx tests; typecheck+build green; RED-is-real; nothing built for the deferred scope.

gate.prompt: All three judges return at_least_as_good=true with zero must-fix findings.

retro.lesson: Have multiple models independently confirm new code + tests are at least as good as the surrounding code before a friend's-repo PR. Calibrate the gate: it must go RED once on a deliberately-worse patch before it is trusted.

steps.description: Human gate: browser walkthrough with the user of the built drawer against the AC checklist. THE AUTONOMOUS RUN STOPS HERE and waits for the human.
Retrospect the slice; append learnings to the ledger.
NEW — open the PR against main with unit tests, the completed AC checklist + walkthrough evidence, and the quality-panel verdict attached. No version bump / tag (maintainer-cut only).
